requirement: Build secure credit card payment service compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Credit Card Payment Service
prd.summary: A service to process credit card payments securely, adhering to PCI-DSS requirements.
functional_requirements: Accept credit card payments via API
Tokenize card data to avoid storing raw PAN
Maintain immutable audit logs for all payment actions
non_functional_requirements: Scalable to handle up to 10,000 transactions per second
Response time less than 200 ms for authorization requests
Encryption at rest and in transit
Audit trail retention for at least 12 months
Disaster recovery with RPO < 5 minutes and RTO < 30 minutes
assumptions: Merchants will provision API credentials securely
Acquiring banks support standard ISO 8583 or API protocols
Infrastructure runs within a VPC with controlled network access
Compliance team will provide periodic PCI-DSS validation reports

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Handles inbound API requests, performs request validation, rate limiting, and routes traffic to backend services.

architecture.name: Payment Service
architecture.description: Core business logic for payment processing, tokenization, transaction lifecycle management, and integration with acquiring banks.

architecture.name: Secure Data Store
architecture.description: Encrypted storage for transaction records, tokenized card data, and audit logs, using AES-256 at rest.

architecture.name: Compliance Monitoring Service
architecture.description: Continuously monitors PCI-DSS compliance controls, generates alerts, and provides compliance reporting dashboards.

security_requirements.description: All cardholder data must be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
Sensitive data must be stored encrypted with AES-256.
Access to cardholder data must be restricted to authorized personnel via role‑based access control.

requirement: Build secure credit card payment service compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Credit Card Payment Service
prd.summary: A service to process credit card transactions securely, complying with PCI-DSS standards.
non_functional_requirements: Scalability to handle peak transaction volumes
Encryption of data at rest and in transit
Data retention policies per regulatory requirements
assumptions: Customers provide valid card data
Network connectivity is reliable
Third‑party acquirers are PCI‑DSS compliant
Regulatory environment remains stable

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Handles external client requests, enforces TLS, rate limiting, and routing to internal services.

architecture.name: Payment Processor Service
architecture.description: Core service that authorizes, captures, refunds, and settles credit card transactions.

architecture.name: Tokenization Service
architecture.description: Replaces PANs with secure tokens, stores tokens in a PCI‑DSS compliant vault.

architecture.name: Secure Database
architecture.description: Encrypted relational database for transaction records, token mappings, and audit logs.

architecture.name: Logging Service
architecture.description: Centralized, tamper‑evident log aggregation for all security‑relevant events.

architecture.name: Monitoring & Alerting
architecture.description: Real‑time health checks, performance metrics, and security anomaly detection.

architecture.name: Compliance Service
architecture.description: Ensures ongoing PCI‑DSS controls, runs required reports, and triggers compliance checks.

security_requirements.description: Encrypt all cardholder data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
Encrypt stored cardholder data using AES‑256 with rotating keys.
Implement role‑based access control and least privilege for all system components.
Maintain immutable audit logs for all access to cardholder data.
Perform quarterly vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.
Require multi‑factor authentication for all administrative access.

threats.description: Attacker impersonates a legitimate merchant to submit fraudulent payment requests via the API Gateway.
Malicious actor modifies transaction data in transit between API Gateway and Payment Processor Service.
Customer denies initiating a transaction due to insufficient non‑repudiation logs.
Sensitive card data leaked from logs if the Logging Service fails to redact PANs.
Attacker overwhelms the API Gateway with high‑volume requests, causing service outage.
Compromised credentials for the Tokenization Service allow an attacker to generate valid payment tokens.

requirement: Build secure payment gateway for credit card processing compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Credit Card Payment Gateway
prd.summary: A system to process credit card payments securely, meeting PCI-DSS compliance.
functional_requirements: Accept credit card details via web/mobile
non_functional_requirements: Data encryption at rest and in transit
assumptions: Acquiring bank provides API
Customers have internet access
PCI DSS compliance scope limited to card data handling
Infrastructure hosted in cloud with VPC

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Routes incoming payment requests, enforces TLS, rate limiting.

architecture.name: Payment Service
architecture.description: Core business logic for tokenization, authorization, refunds.

architecture.name: Token Vault
architecture.description: Secure storage for tokenized card data, encrypted at rest.

architecture.name: Encryption Service
architecture.description: Handles encryption/decryption of sensitive data using HSM.

architecture.name: Audit Log Service
architecture.description: Immutable logging of all payment events for compliance.

architecture.name: 3D Secure Service
architecture.description: Integrates with 3DS provider for authentication.

security_requirements.description: All cardholder data must be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
Cardholder data must never be stored in plaintext; use tokenization.
Access to production systems must be restricted to authorized personnel with MFA.
Audit logs must be tamper‑evident and retained for at least one year.

threats.description: Attacker impersonates a legitimate customer to submit fraudulent payment requests through the API Gateway.
Malicious actor modifies payment request payload (e.g., amount or merchant ID) in transit before it reaches the Payment Service.
User denies having initiated a transaction, and insufficient non‑repudiation controls allow the claim to go unchecked.
Sensitive card data or tokens are intercepted from the API Gateway or Token Vault due to inadequate encryption or misconfigured access controls.
Adversary floods the API Gateway with high‑volume requests, exhausting resources and causing service unavailability.
Exploitation of a vulnerability in the Payment Service or Encryption Service grants the attacker administrative privileges over token management.

requirement: Build secure payment gateway for credit card processing compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Credit Card Payment Gateway
prd.summary: A payment gateway that processes credit card transactions securely, meeting PCI-DSS compliance.
non_functional_requirements: Encryption at rest and in transit
assumptions: Merchant provides API keys
Network connectivity is reliable
Third-party fraud detection service available

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Routes incoming payment requests to appropriate services and enforces TLS termination.

architecture.name: Payment Processor Service
architecture.description: Handles authorization, capture, refund, and void operations with acquiring banks.

architecture.name: Tokenization Service
architecture.description: Replaces PAN with reversible tokens; stores tokens securely and never persists raw card data.

architecture.name: Secure Database
architecture.description: Encrypted storage for transaction logs, token mappings, and audit records.

architecture.name: Logging & Monitoring Service
architecture.description: Collects immutable logs, forwards to SIEM, and provides real‑time alerts for suspicious activity.

security_requirements.description: Encrypt all cardholder data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
Never store full PAN; use tokenization and store only the last four digits.
Enforce strict role‑based access control and MFA for all administrative interfaces.
Maintain immutable audit logs for all access to cardholder data.
Perform regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing as required by PCI-DSS.

threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate user by stealing credentials or session tokens.
Malicious actor may modify transaction data in transit if TLS is bypassed.
User could deny initiating a fund transfer without proper non‑repudiation logs.
Sensitive personal and financial data could be exposed through insecure API endpoints.
Attackers could overwhelm the API gateway, causing service outage.
A compromised low‑privilege component could gain admin rights to the transaction service.

requirement: Build secure payment gateway for credit card processing compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Credit Card Payment Gateway
prd.summary: A system to process credit card payments securely, meeting PCI-DSS compliance.
functional_requirements: Accept credit card details via API
non_functional_requirements: Audit logging of all payment events
Data encryption at rest and in transit

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Routes incoming payment requests, enforces rate limiting and TLS termination.

architecture.name: Payment Processor Service
architecture.description: Core service that validates, tokenizes, and forwards transaction data to the acquiring bank.

architecture.name: Tokenization Service
architecture.description: Generates and stores PCI‑DSS compliant payment tokens, ensuring raw card data never persists.

architecture.name: Database

security_requirements.description: All cardholder data must be encrypted using AES‑256 in storage.
All network communication must use TLS 1.3 with strong cipher suites.
Access to production systems must be restricted to authorized personnel with MFA.
Audit logs must be immutable and retained for at least one year.
Vulnerability scanning and penetration testing must be performed quarterly.

threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate user to initiate unauthorized payments.
Modification of payment request parameters could alter transaction amounts.
Lack of proper logging may allow users to deny having made a payment.
Sensitive cardholder data could be exposed through insecure storage or logs.
An attacker could overwhelm the API service, preventing legitimate transactions.
Exploiting a vulnerability in the API could grant an attacker admin-level access.

requirement: Build secure payment gateway for credit card processing compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Payment Gateway for Credit Card Processing
prd.summary: A payment gateway that securely processes credit card transactions while meeting PCI-DSS compliance requirements.
functional_requirements: Accept credit card payments via API and web UI
Validate card details and perform authorization with acquiring banks
Tokenize and store card data securely
Support refunds, voids, and recurring billing
Provide real-time transaction status and reporting
non_functional_requirements: Scalable to handle peak transaction volumes
assumptions: Acquiring banks provide standard ISO 8583 interfaces
Customers will use TLS 1.2+ for all communications
Infrastructure runs in a cloud environment with dedicated VPC

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Handles external HTTPS traffic, terminates TLS, performs request routing and rate limiting.

architecture.name: Payment Processor Service
architecture.description: Core service that validates requests, interacts with acquiring banks, and orchestrates transaction flow.

architecture.name: Tokenization Service
architecture.description: Generates and manages payment tokens, ensuring raw card data never persists in plaintext.

architecture.name: Secure Database
architecture.description: Encrypted storage for transaction logs, token mappings, and audit records, using FIPS‑validated encryption.

architecture.name: Fraud Detection Engine
architecture.description: Analyzes transaction patterns in real time to flag suspicious activity.

architecture.name: Monitoring & Alerting
architecture.description: Collects metrics, logs, and security events; triggers alerts for anomalies and compliance violations.

security_requirements.description: All cardholder data must be encrypted at rest using AES‑256 with keys managed by an HSM.
All network communication must use TLS 1.2 or higher with strong cipher suites.
Access to production systems must be restricted to authorized personnel via multi‑factor authentication.
Audit logs must be immutable and retained for at least 12 months.
Vulnerability scanning and penetration testing must be performed quarterly.

requirement: Build secure payment gateway for credit card processing compliant with PCI-DSS

prd.title: Secure Payment Gateway for Credit Card Processing
prd.summary: A system to process credit card transactions securely, meeting PCI-DSS compliance.
non_functional_requirements: Scalable to handle peak transaction volumes
Low latency processing (< 200 ms per transaction)
Encryption of data at rest and in transit
Strict access control and role‑based permissions
assumptions: Merchants will supply valid API credentials
Network connectivity to acquiring banks and card networks is reliable
PCI‑DSS scope and responsibilities are defined by the merchant
Third‑party fraud detection services are available via APIs

architecture.name: API Gateway
architecture.description: Handles external merchant requests, performs TLS termination, rate limiting, and routing to internal services.

architecture.name: Payment Processor Service
architecture.description: Core service that validates requests, orchestrates authorization with card networks, and records transaction outcomes.

architecture.name: Tokenization Service
architecture.description: Generates and manages reversible tokens for PANs, ensuring no raw card data is stored.

architecture.name: Secure Database
architecture.description: Encrypted storage for transaction metadata, token mappings, and audit logs, with strict access controls.

architecture.name: Logging & Monitoring Service
architecture.description: Collects immutable logs, forwards to SIEM, and provides real‑time alerts for security events.

architecture.name: Compliance Monitoring Module
architecture.description: Continuously validates system configuration against PCI‑DSS requirements and generates compliance reports.

security_requirements.description: All communication must use TLS 1.2 or higher with strong cipher suites.
Never store raw PAN; use tokenization for any persisted card data.
Encrypt sensitive data at rest using AES‑256.
Implement role‑based access control and least‑privilege principles for all system components.
Maintain immutable audit logs for all access to cardholder data and security‑relevant events.

threats.description: An attacker could impersonate a legitimate user by stealing credentials or session tokens.
Modification of transaction data in transit if TLS is improperly configured.
Insufficient logging could allow users to deny performed transactions.
Sensitive personal and financial data could be exposed through insecure API endpoints.
An attacker could overwhelm the API gateway with excessive requests, causing service outage.
Exploiting a flaw in the authentication flow could grant attacker admin rights.
